title: runtime assert error r=ERROR: Assert `count_id(wire->name) == 0' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:2022. e=
body:
After a long time (20h cpu time on x86_64  AMD EPYC 7601, linux debian bookworm/sid) at 52.2GB memory footprint in a final assembly run for one verilog and 605  just-synthesized json files with xilinx target, this assert error occurred:

r=ERROR: Assert `count_id(wire->name) == 0' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:2022.

It is from here:

void RTLIL::Module::add(RTLIL::Wire *wire)
{
        log_assert(!wire->name.empty());
        log_assert(count_id(wire->name) == 0);        <--- HERE
        log_assert(refcount_wires_ == 0);
        wires_[wire->name] = wire;
        wire->module = this;
}


It occurred here in the synthesis process:

...
607.18.3. Continuing TECHMAP pass.
No more expansions possible.
<suppressed ~5 debug messages>
607.19. Executing OPT_EXPR pass (perform const folding).
607.20. Executing WREDUCE pass (reducing word size of cells).
607.21. Executing XILINX_DSP pass (pack resources into DSPs).
Analysing Synth_KPU_kpu32_aluA.$mul$Synth_KPU_kpu32_aluA.v:1122$862.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.sliceA.last.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.sliceB[0].mul for Xilinx DSP packing.
...
 Analysing Synth_KPU_kpu32_fpuA2.$mul$Synth_KPU_kpu32_fpuA2.v:1472$32015.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.mul_sliceB_last for Xilinx DSP packing.
Analysing Synth_KPU_kpu32_fpuA2.$mul$Synth_KPU_kpu32_fpuA2.v:2504$32281.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.m
****** HERE *****

The yosys program being executed was

read_json *.json; read_verilog KPU.v; synth_xilinx -top KPU; write_json KPU.json

The json files had previously been generated by applying the same process to the verilog files for each of the submodules, and then editing out all library module inclusions and any repeated submodule inclusions across different files. 

(I would be grateful if somebody could tell me yosys commands to ONLY produce json for the top level module selected with -top)

The aim of this yosys procedure is incremental synthesis (ditto, please). It has been tried successfully on smaller assemblies. It saves on peak memory requirement by a factor of two.

Oh yes ... this is Yosys 0.23 (git sha1 7ce5011c24b)


I GUESS that this is a wrap round on some count variable for number of wires allocated, or not wiping the identifier on a freed wire that is later reallocated, causing the assert fail.

Or it could be an underlying malloc error or system error or hardware error.

I have the files and could repeat the experiment, but that would take about 10 days wall clock time.

Does this ring a bell with anyone?

Regards

PTB
